summary: HIV-infected patients have an increased risk of silent myocardial ischemia due to comorbidities associated with both infection and treatment, particularly when aging or having other metabolic disorders. Gated myocardial perfusion SPECT can detect occult ischemia before symptomatic presentation, offering significant potential benefits in improving early diagnosis and reducing future morbidity/mortality among these high-risk individuals.
